overview: The Public Works Committee approved several key items including a resolution supporting state transportation funding, awarded a recycled asphalt crushing contract to King Gravel for $126,900, and directed staff to develop enforcement language for sanitary sewer discharge violations at $100/day. The committee also approved construction agreements for the Military Road project and construction services for upcoming road projects.

agenda.item: Acknowledge April 2026 water and sewer permits
Review 2025 Water Utility Public Service Commission annual reports
Water and sewer connection agreement for Radkey Avenue duplex
Construction service proposals for Alderson/Jelnik and Bladel Avenue projects

discussions.body: Michael reported the village received a Local Road Improvement Grant for Birch Street reconstruction between Schofield Avenue and Community Center Drive, originally a 2029 project that will likely move to 2028. The grant covers about 50% of the estimated $1.5 million project cost. Michael noted the village has received over $9 million in grant funding over the past 4-5 years. Discussion also covered Weston Avenue repairs by Integrity Grading and Excavating for settlement issues over sanitary sewer trenches, and upcoming repairs on Schofield Avenue by HOS Construction for manhole and pavement issues.

Staff reported on removal of road limits on April 29th, spring curb line sweeping, hot mix patching, storm damage cleanup from East Nick, culvert failures on Jones Street requiring additional 18-inch culvert installation, and equipment maintenance including transitioning plow trucks for summer use. Discussion addressed flooding issues near Weston Avenue and the need for a potential permanent pump solution near Crane Meadows area.

Josh reported on water service repairs, well maintenance, and significant clear water infiltration issues in the sanitary system. In April, the village put 37 million gallons of water into the distribution system but treated nearly 54 million gallons at the wastewater facility - approximately 15 million gallons of clear water from infiltration or illegal sump pump connections. The Kathleen lift station was identified as having issues with residents discharging sump pumps into sanitary sewers.

Michael presented the annual report showing operating revenues of approximately $3 million, net operating income of $482,911 (compared to $490,000 in 2024), and water loss at 6%. The utility value increased to nearly $34 million with $6.7 million in infrastructure placed in service. The utility has approximately 6,100 customers total: 5,250 in Weston, 830 in Rothschild, and 5 in other areas. Approved unanimously.

Michael explained this League of Wisconsin Municipalities sample resolution supports long-term sustainable funding for state transportation, noting that while grant funding helps one-time costs, recurring annual payments for street maintenance and snow plowing remain underfunded. Motion by Mumper to recommend adoption, seconded, approved unanimously.

Michael explained the Ryan Street site recycling program and presented quotes, with King Gravel as low bidder at $4.23/ton for an estimated 30,000 tons totaling $126,900. Analysis showed the village saves approximately $11 per cubic yard compared to City of Wausau costs, resulting in about $130,000 savings on this year's three projects. Motion approved unanimously to award to King Gravel.

Discussion focused on establishing enforcement for illegal sump pump discharges into sanitary sewers. Staff sought guidance on fine amounts within the $100-$1,000 per day range allowed by code. Committee members distinguished between unknowing and willful violations, agreeing that $100/day is appropriate for first offenses. Motion approved to direct staff to create enforcement language not to exceed $100/day.

Michael explained a builder needs to connect a duplex on lot one to water and sewer mains that currently dead-end on Radkey Avenue. The proposal allows temporary long lateral service until Radkey Avenue reconstruction, when the property would be assessed for main extension costs. Committee added language requiring reconstruction no later than 2029. Motion approved unanimously.

Michael presented an intergovernmental agreement with Village of Rothschild for Military Road construction from Wolfman Street to Helin Street. Steam Construction is the contractor. Weston's share for utility relocation (water main and sanitary sewer) is estimated at $94,860. The water main requires relocation due to conflicts with storm sewer improvements. Motion approved unanimously.

Michael presented proposals for construction inspection services for upcoming projects. For Alderson/Jelnik roundabout, MSA was lower at $37,315 vs Clark Dietz at $48,910. For Bladel Avenue, MSA was $70,400 and Clark Dietz was $73,730. Michael recommended MSA for the roundabout and Clark Dietz for Bladel due to having full-time experienced staff versus an intern. Motion approved unanimously.

actionItems: Approve Resolution 2026-014 supporting state transportation funding (to Village Board)
Award recycled asphalt crushing contract to King Gravel at $4.23/ton, estimated $126,900
Create enforcement language for sanitary sewer discharge violations at $100/day maximum
Execute water/sewer connection agreement for 2230 Radkey Avenue with 2029 reconstruction deadline
Approve Military Road construction agreement with Village of Rothschild for $94,860
Award construction services: MSA for Alderson/Jelnik project ($37,315), Clark Dietz for Bladel Avenue ($73,730)
Special assessment meeting for Bladel Avenue scheduled May 28, 2026 at 6:00 PM
Staff to research cost impact of clear water infiltration for next meeting
Staff to provide street lighting update for future meeting
Norcon to begin Schofield Avenue concrete repairs Wednesday/Thursday this week
title: Public Works Committee
